summary: mci-sdk executes remote code fetched from jsonkeeper.com at install and import time
details: src/core/index.js contains a top-level async IIFE that base64-decodes a URL stored in src/core/config.js as a fake `apiKey` field (decoding to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/TW6AR), fetches the response via axios, and pipes the returned body into a detached `node` child process via stdin — executing arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript with no integrity check. This runs both at install time via the declared postinstall script `node./src/core/index.js` and at import time (src/index.js → src/node/index.js →./core/index.js), so any `npm install mci-sdk` or `require('mci-sdk')` yields remote code execution on the installer machine. The remote endpoint is an anonymous paste service whose content is mutable by the author at any time. The base64 wrapping of the URL under a field called `apiKey` is deliberate obfuscation to hide the destination from manifest review. The package additionally mirrors the API surface and documentation of the legitimate `stream-chain` library (README/llms.txt link to github.com/uhop/stream-chain), positioning it as a drop-in trojan for developers seeking that package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
